questions.stem: Should environmental clearance for coal mines be valid for the entire mine life? Arg I: Yes, reduces bureaucratic delays. Arg II: No, periodic review ensures compliance.

questions.stem: Find the wrong term: AD, EH, IL, MP, QT

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statement: A recent survey shows that 80% of mining engineers prefer underground mining over opencast. Conclusion I: Underground mining offers better career growth. II: Most mining engineers dislike opencast mining.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: How many times from 6 AM to 6 PM are hands at right angle?

questions.stem: If 'A @ B' means A is mother of B, 'A # B' means A is father of B, 'A $ B' means A is sister of B. In 'P @ Q $ R # S', how is P related to S?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A, B, C, D, E sit in a line. A is not at ends. B is right of A but not at end. C is left of D. E is at left end. Who is in middle?

questions.stem: Statements: All birds can fly. Some creatures that fly are bats. All bats are mammals. Conclusions: I. Some birds are bats. II. Some mammals are not birds.

questions.stem: Find the wrong term: 1, 3, 6, 10, 15, 21, 28, 37

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of INNOCUOUS:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Inflation reduces the purchasing power of money. Q: Central banks adjust interest rates to control inflation. R: High inflation can erode savings and reduce living standards. S: Moderate inflation is considered healthy for economic growth.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of OSTENTATIOUS:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Urbanisation is increasing rapidly worldwide. Q: People migrate to cities in search of better opportunities. R: This puts pressure on housing and infrastructure. S: Sustainable urban planning is needed to manage growth.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of ADMONISH:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Stock markets reflect the health of an economy. Q: Share prices fluctuate based on supply and demand. R: Investors buy and sell shares of publicly listed companies. S: Market regulators ensure fair trading practices.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of COMPLACENT:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Globalisation has connected economies around the world. Q: International trade has grown exponentially. R: Multinational corporations operate across borders. S: Critics argue that globalisation increases inequality.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of SPURIOUS:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: The Amazon rainforest is often called the lungs of the Earth. Q: It produces about 20% of the world's oxygen. R: Deforestation rates have increased in recent decades. S: International efforts are underway to protect the rainforest.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of LACONIC:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Natural disasters cause widespread destruction. Q: Early warning systems can reduce the impact. R: Preparedness and planning are essential. S: Governments invest in disaster management infrastructure.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of PLACID:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Vaccines have saved millions of lives worldwide. Q: They work by training the immune system to fight diseases. R: Herd immunity protects those who cannot be vaccinated. S: Vaccine development requires rigorous clinical trials.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of FORTIFY:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: The Green Revolution increased agricultural productivity. Q: High-yield crop varieties were developed and introduced. R: Chemical fertilisers and pesticides were used extensively. S: This helped many countries achieve food security.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of ALOOF:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The manager was angry ____ the delay.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The region ____ from frequent power outages.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To pull someone's leg':

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of JEOPARDIZE:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To burn the midnight oil':
options: To work late into the night
questions.solution: To work late into the night

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of JUBILANT:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of PERSEVERANCE:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of CATALYST:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Electron Spin Resonance (ESR) dating:
options: Trapped electrons in crystal defects. Non-destructive. For tooth enamel, quartz
questions.solution: ESR: analog of luminescence. Detects trapped charge. Combined with U-series for teeth.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Landslide risk assessment considers:
questions.solution: Landslide risk: combination of hazard (trigger), exposure (infrastructure), vulnerability. Multi-factor analysis required.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: P-T path determination from garnet zoning and inclusions:
options: Garnet core = early P-T. Rim = peak conditions. Inclusions record path
questions.solution: Garnet: chronometer. Inclusion assemblages at different distances from core = different P-T. Thermobarometry.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Migmatite represents:
questions.solution: Migamatite: mixed rock (leucosome + melanosome). Represents partial melting at deep crustal levels. Transition to igneous.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Delhi Supergroup:
options: Proterozoic (1.8-0.8 Ga). Alwar + Ajabgarh + Gogunda. Folded into Delhi fold belt
questions.solution: Delhi: NE-SW trending fold belt. Marble, quartzite, schist. Built of Delhi (Red Fort).

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Braided stream pattern indicates:
options: High sediment load and variable discharge
questions.solution: Braided: multiple channels, high load, variable flow. Common in glacial outwash, arid regions, steep gradients.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Shear strength of rocks:
options: Coulomb criterion: τ = c + σ·tan(φ). c = cohesion, φ = friction angle
questions.solution: Brittle: Hoek-Brown criterion. Rock joints: Barton-Bandis (JRC, JCS).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ductile shear zone types — by offset:
options: Progressive displacement. Normal, reverse, strike-slip. Strain gradient from wall to center
questions.solution: Shear zones: mylonite series (S-C mylonite → ultramylonite → pseudotachylyte).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Karst — doline (sinkhole):
options: Closed depression. Solution vs collapse doline. Uvala: coalescing dolines
questions.solution: Doline: funnel-shaped. Polje: large flat-floored depression. Karst plain: lapies + dolines.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: ARC basalt — flux melting:
options: Fluid from slab → mantle wedge melting. Hydrous → low F. High large-ion lithophile (LIL) / Nb-Ta negative anomaly
questions.solution: Arc basalt: elevated Ba/La, low Nb/La. Subduction signature. Adakite: slab melting.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Infiltration — Green-Ampt:
options: Two-stage: initial (high rate) → steady state (f_c). Wetting front, suction head
questions.solution: Green-Ampt: f = K_s·(1 + ψ·Δθ/F). F = cumulative infiltration. ψ = suction at wetting front.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Bravais lattices — 14 types:
options: Cubic (P, I, F), Tetragonal (P, I), Orthorhombic (P, C, I, F), Hexagonal (P), Trigonal (R), Monoclinic (P, C), Triclinic (P)
questions.solution: Bravais: 14 space lattices. Crystal systems: 7. Point groups: 32. Space groups: 230.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The fold shown in cross-section (beds sag downward about the axial plane) is a(n):
questions.solution: Downward-sagging limbs dipping toward the axis define a syncline (youngest beds in core).

figure.caption: syncline (cross-section)

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Lower mantle minerals: bridgmanite (Mg,Fe)SiO₃:
options: Perovskite structure. Most abundant mineral in Earth by volume
questions.solution: Bridgmanite: perovskite structure. ~75% of lower mantle. Discovered in meteorite (Tenham).

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Refractive index of diamond is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rock retains 25% of its original parent isotope (half-life 0.7 Gyr). Its radiometric age is about:

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Acid mine drainage has pH typically:
questions.solution: AMD: sulfide minerals oxidize to produce sulfuric acid. pH can be <3. Devastating to aquatic ecosystems. Treatment expensive.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Magnetotelluric method uses:
questions.solution: MT: uses natural EM fields (solar wind, lightning). Measures E and H at surface. Images subsurface resistivity.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Sequence boundary (SB) represents:
questions.solution: SB: erosion surface from subaerial exposure during sea-level fall. Separates sequences. Key sequence stratigraphy surface.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Chalk:
questions.solution: Chalk: White Cliffs of Dover. Pure CaCO3. Used in cement, lime.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Pressure shadow mineral growth indicates:
questions.solution: Pressure shadows: fibrous mineral growth on rigid porphyroclast ends. Indicates ductile flow. Fibers track deformation path.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Porphyry copper deposit:
options: Low-grade, large-tonnage. Fracture-controlled Cu-Fe sulfides in porphyritic intrusion
questions.solution: Porphyry Cu: stockwork veins. Chalcopyrite, bornite. Largest Cu source globally.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fault — slickenlines:
options: Linear scratches on fault surface. Indicate slip direction. Steps show sense
questions.solution: Slickenlines: calcite/quartz fibers. Steps: Riedel shears. Sense from stepping direction.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Lithosphere-asthenosphere boundary (LAB):
options: Rheological boundary. Mechanical lithosphere ~100 km (continental) to ~50 km (oceanic). Seismic low-velocity zone
questions.solution: LAB: seismic discontinuity. Thermal: ~1300°C isotherm. Electrical conductivity change.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Diagenetic environments:
questions.solution: Eogenesis: vadose/meteoric. Mesogenesis: compaction, cementation. Telogenesis: dissolution, fracturing.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Image fusion (pan-sharpening):
options: Merge high-res panchromatic with low-res multispectral. Brovey, IHS, PCA, Gram-Schmidt
questions.solution: Pan-sharp: 0.3m pan + 1.2m MS → 0.3m MS. Spectral distortion vs spatial improvement tradeoff.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 6 m seam over 6 km² (density 1.4 t/m³) has a recovery factor of 0.85. The mineable reserve is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Plate boundary types:
options: Divergent (mid-ocean ridge), Convergent (subduction, collision), Transform (conservative). Triple junctions
questions.solution: Triple junctions: RRR (Afar), TTT (Japan), FFF (San Andreas). Hess (1962) — seafloor spreading.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Chromite ore genesis in ophiolites:
options: Podiform: mantle section. Stratiform: layered intrusion. Cr/(Cr+Al) indicates tectonic setting
questions.solution: Podiform: mantle, harzburgite host. Stratiform: layered mafic intrusion. Cr#: higher = more depleted mantle.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Karewa Group:
options: Pleistocene lake deposits in Kashmir. Fluvio-glacial. Loess interbeds. Blue clay
questions.solution: Karewa: 1500m thick. Plant fossils, pollen. Paleoclimate archive. Saffron cultivation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: LiDAR return types: first return = canopy top. Last return =:
questions.solution: LiDAR: multiple returns. First = top. Last = ground (if penetrates canopy). Bare earth model.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Inselberg:
options: Isolated bedrock hill in arid/semiarid plains. Bornhardt (granite), kopje
questions.solution: Inselberg: weathering remnant. Pediment: gently sloping bedrock surface around inselberg.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hardness of quartz on Mohs scale:
questions.solution: Quartz (SiO2) — hardness 7. Scratches glass. Common in all rock types.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Corundum vs spinel in thin section:
options: Corundum: high relief, hexagonal, extreme birefringence. Spinel: cubic, isotropic
questions.solution: Corundum: n=1.762-1.770, δ=0.008. Spinel: n=1.712-1.736, isotropic.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The chemical composition of quartz is:
questions.solution: Quartz is silicon dioxide, SiO₂, one of the commonest minerals.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Mineral fluorescence under UV light:
options: Short-wave (254nm) vs long-wave (366nm). Calcite, fluorite, willemite fluoresce
questions.solution: Fluorite: blue (SW). Calcite: red, pink. Willemite: green (SW). Used in mineral identification.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Mica group — muscovite:
questions.solution: Muscovite: white mica. Dioctahedral (2 Al per formula unit). Common in granite.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fluvial response to climate change:
options: Glacial (braided, outwash) vs interglacial (meandering, floodplain). MIS stages shift patterns
questions.solution: Climate: wet/dry cycles shift sediment load. Terrace formation during transitions.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Spectral signature:
options: Reflectance vs wavelength. Different materials have unique signatures
questions.solution: Vegetation: high NIR reflectance. Water: low reflectance. Minerals: absorption features at specific wavelengths.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Steel sets (tunnel support):
options: Rib supports (I-beams) in weak ground. Wood blocking between steel and rock
questions.solution: Steel sets: used in heavy squeezing/loose ground. Lattice girders. Forepoling.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Obsidian:
options: Volcanic glass (amorphous, no crystals). Conchoidal fracture
questions.solution: Obsidian: rapid cooling of felsic lava. Used for tools by ancient humans.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Permian-Triassic extinction (~252 Ma):
options: Largest extinction (96% species). Siberian Traps. Ocean anoxia. H2S
questions.solution: P-Tr: 'Great Dying'. Volcanic CO2 → warming → anoxia. Terrestrial: fungi spike.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Clastic vs non-clastic:
options: Clastic: fragments of pre-existing rocks. Non-clastic: chemical/biochemical precipitation
questions.solution: Clastic: conglomerate, sandstone, shale. Non-clastic: limestone, evaporite.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Amphibolite:
options: Hornblende + plagioclase. Medium-high grade. Equivalent to basalt/gabbro
questions.solution: Amphibolite: medium to high grade (amphibolite facies). No pyroxene (granulite has pyroxene).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Groundwater geophysics — NMR:
options: Nuclear magnetic resonance (MRS). Direct detection of groundwater. T₂* decay time for pore size
questions.solution: MRS: surface NMR. Water content ε, decay T₂*. Logging NMR also. Porosity + permeability estimate.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An ore body of 2 Mt has an average metal grade of 1%. The contained metal is about:
questions.solution: Metal = tonnage × grade = 2 Mt × 1% = 20 thousand tonnes (kt).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rock retains 50% of its original parent isotope (half-life 0.7 Gyr). Its radiometric age is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fold axis plunge:
options: Angle of fold hinge line from horizontal
questions.solution: Plunging fold: hinge line inclined. Non-plunging: horizontal hinge.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal sample (proximate analysis) has 4% moisture, 25% volatile matter and 8% ash. Its fixed carbon is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Slug test:
options: Instantaneous change in water level. For low K aquifers. Hvorslev, Bouwer-Rice
questions.solution: Slug test: quick, inexpensive. T estimate for monitoring wells. Near-well conditions.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hardness of feldspar on Mohs scale:
questions.solution: Feldspar — hardness 6. Most abundant mineral group in Earth's crust.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: River profile — Knickpoint:
options: Break in river longitudinal profile (waterfall, rapids). Indicates rejuvenation or lithological control
questions.solution: Knickpoint: upstream migration by headward erosion. Base level change indicator.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Spaceborne sensors — MODIS:
options: 36 bands, 250-1000m. 1-2 day revisit. 2330km swath. Global monitoring
questions.solution: MODIS: Terra (AM) + Aqua (PM). NDVI, SST, fire, snow cover, land cover. Daily global.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Coulomb's earth pressure theory:
options: Considers wall friction and sloping backfill. Wedge analysis
questions.solution: Coulomb: wedge trial. Wall friction δ reduces active pressure. P_a = ½γH²K_a.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Phaneritic texture:
questions.solution: Phaneritic: crystals >1mm. Slow cooling at depth.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Great Unconformity (Grand Canyon):
options: Proterozoic Vishnu Schist overlain by Cambrian Tapeats Sandstone
questions.solution: Great Unconformity: ~1.2 Ga gap. Vishnu (1.7 Ga) → Tapeats (525 Ma). Angular + nonconformity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Provenance in sedimentary geology:
options: Source area of sediments (composition, age, tectonic setting)
questions.solution: Provenance: heavy mineral analysis, zircon U-Pb dating, paleocurrents.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Eclogite facies:
options: Garnet + omphacite. Very high P. Densest crustal rock (~3.5 g/cm³)
questions.solution: Eclogite: subduction zone. Coesite in UHP rocks. Diamond-grade: >4 GPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Potential field data: upward continuation:
options: Transforms anomaly from measurement height to higher elevation. Enhances deep sources
questions.solution: Upward continuation: low-pass filter. Deeper sources preserved, shallow attenuated.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Uranium deposits — unconformity-related:
options: Athabasca Basin (Canada). High grade (>1% U₃O₈). Basement + sandstone. U/Pb age ~1.5 Ga
questions.solution: Unconformity: massive U within altered basement. Cigar Lake, McArthur River.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A mineral has passed through 1 half-lives of an isotope whose half-life is 5.73 (in the given time units). Its age is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the proximate analysis of coal, fixed carbon is obtained by subtracting from 100% the sum of:
questions.solution: Fixed carbon (%) = 100 − (moisture + volatile matter + ash) in proximate analysis.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Crystal system of andalusite:
questions.solution: Andalusite (Al2SiO5) — prismatic, square cross-section. Chiastolite variety has cross pattern.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Remote sensing definition:
options: Acquiring information about Earth without physical contact (satellite, aircraft)
questions.solution: RS: electromagnetic radiation interaction with Earth surface. Passive (solar) and active (radar).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Aphanitic texture:
questions.solution: Aphanitic: crystals not visible. Rapid cooling at surface.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Coal:
options: Organic sedimentary rock (plant remains). Rank: peat→lignite→bituminous→anthracite
questions.solution: Coal: swamp environments. Peat (low rank) → Anthracite (high rank, high C).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fault definition:
options: Fracture with displacement. Hanging wall (above) vs footwall (below)
questions.solution: Hanging wall: block above fault plane. Footwall: block below.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Streak of magnetite:
questions.solution: Magnetite (Fe3O4) — strongly magnetic. Black streak. Important iron ore.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Scoria:
options: Vesicular mafic volcanic rock (darker, denser than pumice)
questions.solution: Scoria: basalt with vesicles. Cinder cones. Heavier than pumice.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal sample (proximate analysis) has 10% moisture, 20% volatile matter and 10% ash. Its fixed carbon is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Geotechnical baseline report (GBR):
options: Defines baseline ground conditions for contractual purposes
questions.solution: GBR: contractual baseline. Used in dispute resolution. FIDIC conditions of contract.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Coal seam correlation methods:
options: Facies analysis, palynology, geochemistry, geophysical logging
questions.solution: Correlation: palynomorph assemblages, geochemical fingerprinting, well logs (density, gamma ray).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Seismic microzonation:
options: Detailed hazard mapping. Amplification, liquefaction, landslide susceptibility
questions.solution: Microzonation: 1:10,000 scale. For urban planning and building codes. Site-specific response analysis.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: PolSAR (polarimetric SAR):
options: Multiple polarization channels (HH, HV, VH, VV). Target characterization
questions.solution: PolSAR: decomposition (Pauli, Freeman-Durden). Crop classification, soil moisture.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Uniaxial compressive strength (UCS) testing: intact rock sample. Granite UCS typically:
questions.solution: Granite: 100-250 MPa. Sandstone: 20-170 MPa. Shale: 5-25 MPa. Basalt: 100-300 MPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Unsteady flow (Theis):
options: s = Q·W(u)/(4πT). u = r²S/(4Tt). W(u) = well function
questions.solution: Theis: non-equilibrium. Type curve matching for T and S estimation. Jacob approximation for small u.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Groundwater modeling: MODFLOW uses:
questions.solution: MODFLOW: USGS code. Block-centered finite difference. Grid cells. Steady or transient.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Kink bands form in:
options: Strongly foliated rocks under compressive stress parallel to foliation
questions.solution: Kink bands: angular folds in well-foliated rocks. Foliation rotates sharply across kink band.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Plagioclase feldspar series:
options: Albite (Na) to Anorthite (Ca) — continuous solid solution

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Porosity n:
options: Void volume/total volume. Primary (intergranular) vs secondary (fracture, solution)
questions.solution: Porosity: sand 25-40%, sandstone 5-30%, granite <1%. Effective porosity: interconnected pores.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The section shows 20 m of overburden above a 3 m coal seam. The volumetric stripping ratio (overburden : coal thickness) is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fault — riedel shears:
options: R, R', P, T, Y shears in strike-slip zone. Indicate shear sense

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A bed dipping at 30° shows a width of 10 m across a horizontal surface. Its true (perpendicular) thickness is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Shotcrete:
options: Sprayed concrete (dry or wet mix). Steel fiber reinforced. Applied to rock surface
questions.solution: Shotcrete: immediate support. Add accelerator. Wire mesh or fiber. 50-100mm thick.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An aquifer (K = 10 m/day) of cross-section 100 m² has a hydraulic gradient of 0.02. The groundwater discharge is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Lineation:
options: Linear fabric element (mineral alignment, intersection, stretching)
questions.solution: Lineation: parallel to fold axes, mineral elongation, or intersection of foliations.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Indo-Gangetic Plain:
options: Foredeep basin. Thick alluvium (Tertiary-Quaternary). Aquifer system

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the faulted block shown, the hanging wall has moved up over the footwall. This is a:
questions.solution: Hanging wall up = reverse fault, caused by compression.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 130" width="260" height="130"><line x1="40" y1="25" x2="150" y2="80" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="20" y="40" width="120" height="70" fill="#fde68a" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1" transform="skewX(-10)"/><rect x="150" y="40" width="100" height="70" fill="#bfdbfe" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="60" y1="55" x2="135" y2="92" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><line x1="160" y1="60" x2="245" y2="60" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><text x="190" y="52" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">hanging wall</text><text x="40" y="35" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">footwall</text></svg>
figure.caption: reverse fault

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Competent vs incompetent layers in folding:
options: Competent layers buckle and are thicker at hinges. Incompetent flow into hinges
questions.solution: Competent: quartzite, sandstone. Incompetent: shale, salt. Parasitic folds in incompetent layers.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Monzonite:
options: Plagioclase ≈ alkali feldspar. Little or no quartz
questions.solution: Monzonite: intermediate between diorite and syenite. Plag=K-spar.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Quartz (hardness 7) will scratch, but not be scratched by, which mineral?
questions.solution: A mineral scratches anything softer; quartz (7) scratches feldspar (6) but not topaz/corundum/diamond.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Laterite formation:
options: Intense chemical weathering under tropical monsoon. Fe/Al enrichment. Bauxite
questions.solution: Laterite: duricrust. Age: Tertiary-Quaternary. Transition: laterite → bauxite (Al-rich).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Groundwater flow equation:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Saltwater intrusion:
options: Freshwater-saltwater interface. Ghyben-Herzberg: z = 40·h (h = head above sea level)
questions.solution: Ghyben-Herzberg: z = ρ_f·h/(ρ_s-ρ_f). For ρ_s/ρ_f = 1.025: z ≈ 40h.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Tunnel boring machine (TBM) selection depends on:
options: Rock mass strength, abrasivity, discontinuities, water inflow
questions.solution: TBM: hard rock (open), soft ground (shielded), mixed face (hybrid). CAI abrasivity index important.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Kimberlite:
options: Ultramafic potassic rock. Source rock for diamonds
questions.solution: Kimberlite: diamond-bearing. Pipe-like bodies. South Africa, Siberia.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Symplectite:
options: Intergrowth of two minerals (e.g., Cpx + Pl) replacing higher-P assemblage. Opx + Cpx + Pl from Grt breakdown
questions.solution: Symplectite: vermicular texture. Decompression reaction. Fingerprint of rapid exhumation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A mineral has passed through 1 half-lives of an isotope whose half-life is 4.5 (in the given time units). Its age is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Clay mineral identification by XRD:
options: Ethylene glycol treatment expands smectite. K-saturation collapses vermiculite
questions.solution: Smectite: expands with glycol (d₀₀₁ 17Å). Illite: no change (10Å). Kaolinite: disappears with K-sat.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 6 m seam over 2 km² (density 1.4 t/m³) has a recovery factor of 0.75. The mineable reserve is about:
